title: eleusine intermedia
intro: Eleusine intermedia, also known as finger grass, is a type of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the leaves of the plant have been used in herbal medicine, boasting medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eleusine intermedia are attributed to its ability to treat various conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
